definition: Current developed-market rate divergence is in the 85th-97th percentile of post-Bretton Woods observations. Fed ~4.25-4.50%, ECB ~2.50-2.75%, BoJ ~0.50%, SNB ~0.25%. Cross-sectional standard deviation of G10 policy rates is ~250-350bp versus a historical mean of ~175bp. The two prior comparable periods (2006-07 and 2018-19) both preceded violent carry reversals.
evidence: Cross-sectional SD of G10 policy rates ~250-350bp vs historical mean ~175bp
97th percentile of cross-sectional SD; top decile of policy rate range
When divergence is top-quartile, probability of 15%+ annual G10 pair move rises from 22% to 33%
Both prior comparable periods (2006-07, 2018-19) preceded carry reversals

VTA framework confirms all four vulnerability conditions: leverage (JPY carry $500B-$4T), maturity mismatch, concentration (single dollar factor = 50-70%), opacity (8x uncertainty band on position size)
Two prior comparable divergence episodes (2006-07, 2018-19) both preceded violent carry reversals
Compressed spring finding: 61% of comparable configurations preceded 20%+ G10 pair move within 12 months vs 11% unconditional (p<0.001)
open_questions: Is the BoJ leg structural or cyclical — and does this change expected FX move magnitude?
Will divergence normalize through rate convergence or through FX adjustment?
Whether resolution occurs via FX adjustment or rate convergence
Whether circuit breakers (central bank pauses, swap lines) can contain the eventual unwind
